name: Sklearn Random Forest Regression
description: Random Forest Regression builds an ensemble of decision trees using bootstrap sampling and feature randomness, and averages their predictions to improve predictive accuracy, robustness, and control overfitting for continuous target variables.

fields.name: targetCol
fields.title: Target Column
fields.description: Target column containing the continuous numeric value that the regression model learns to predict.

fields.name: featureCols
fields.title: Feature Columns
fields.description: List of input feature columns used to train the regression model. All features must be numeric, boolean, or vector-compatible.

fields.name: splitRatio
fields.title: Split Ratio
fields.description: Ratio used to split the dataset into training and testing sets. A value of 1.0 uses the entire dataset for training.

fields.name: n_estimators
fields.title: Number of Estimators
fields.description: Number of decision trees built in the random forest ensemble. Increasing this value generally improves stability but increases computation time.

fields.name: criterion
fields.title: Criterion
fields.description: Metric used to evaluate the quality of a split when growing trees. Different criteria affect how prediction errors are minimized.

fields.name: max_depth
fields.title: Max Depth
fields.description: Maximum depth of each decision tree. Limiting depth reduces overfitting by restricting tree complexity.

fields.name: min_samples_split
fields.title: Min Samples Split
fields.description: Minimum number of samples required to split an internal node. Higher values make trees more conservative and reduce overfitting.

fields.name: min_samples_leaf
fields.title: Min Samples Leaf
fields.description: Minimum number of samples required at a leaf node, ensuring predictions are based on sufficient data.

fields.name: min_weight_fraction_leaf
fields.title: Min Weight Fraction Leaf
fields.description: Minimum weighted fraction of the total sample weight required at a leaf node. Useful when sample weights are provided.

fields.name: max_features
fields.title: Max Features
fields.description: Number of features considered when searching for the best split at each node, promoting diversity among trees.

fields.name: max_leaf_nodes
fields.title: Max Leaf Nodes
fields.description: Maximum number of leaf nodes per tree. Restricting this value limits model complexity.

fields.name: min_impurity_decrease
fields.title: Min Impurity Decrease
fields.description: Minimum impurity reduction required for a node to be split. Higher values lead to simpler trees.

fields.name: bootstrap
fields.title: Bootstrap
fields.description: Determines whether bootstrap sampling is used when building each tree. Disabling uses the full dataset for every tree.

fields.name: oob_score
fields.title: Oob Score
fields.description: Whether to compute out-of-bag R² score using samples not included in bootstrap training.

fields.name: random_state
fields.title: Random State
fields.description: Seed controlling randomness in bootstrap sampling and feature selection, enabling reproducible results.

fields.name: warm_start
fields.title: Warm Start
fields.description: When enabled, reuses previously trained trees and adds additional trees, allowing incremental model training.
